text:
Admission Date: 1980-06-17        Discharge Date: 1996-11-15

Date of Birth:  1980-06-17        Sex:  M

Service:  NB


Please see previous dictated summary dictated 1999-08-19.

HISTORY:  Calderon returned from Charles A Dean Memorial Hospital, status
post bilateral inguinal hernia repair under general
anesthesia.  He had an uneventful surgical course and has
recovered nicely.

REVIEW OF HOSPITAL COURSE BY SYSTEMS:  RESPIRATORY:  Baby is
on room air.  Bilateral breath sounds clear and equal.  Not
having any apnea or bradycardia of prematurity.  He continues
on Diuril for chronic lung disease 28 mg P.O. p. g. every 12
hours.  This equals 30 mg per kilogram per day.  He also is
receiving K-Ciel supplements, 1 mEq P.O. p. g. three times a
day.  This equals 1.5 mEq per kilogram per day.

CARDIOVASCULAR:  The baby is cardiovascularly stable.  No
murmur.  Baseline heart rate 150 to 180s.  Blood pressure
69/41 with a mean of 53.

FLUID, ELECTROLYTES AND NUTRITION:  The baby is feeding
breast milk 34 calories with ProMod at 115 ml per kilo per
day.  Mother is using primarily Barbara Wheeler milk with
supplementation of HMS 4 calories per ounce, MCP 4 calories
per ounce, Polycose 4 calories per ounce, similar powder 2
calories per ounce and ProMod 12-29 teaspoon in 15 ml of breast
milk to which she uses 34 calories per ounce.  The baby is
requiring some gavage feedings, goes to breast when mom is
available and is also P.O. feeding when not available.  He is
also receiving supplemental vitamin E 5 units P.O. p. g. once
daily and ferrous sulfate .15 ml P.O. p. g. once daily of 25
mg per ml.  This equals 2 mg per kilo per day.  Baby is
voiding and stooling without issue.  The last electrolytes on
06-01:  Sodium 133, potassium 6.3, hemolyzed, chloride 101,
CO2 22.  Previous electrolytes on 05-17:  Sodium 131,
potassium 5.1, chloride 97, CO2 24. Weight today is 2215 (inc
55g).

GASTROINTESTINAL:  No current issues.

HEMATOLOGY:  The baby did not require any blood products
intraoperatively or postoperatively.  Hematology - see
previous dictation.

INFECTION DISEASE:  There have been no issues
postoperatively.  See previous dictation.

NEUROLOGY:  The baby is appropriate for gestational age.  See
previous dictation.

SENSORY:  Audiology screening has not been performed at time
of transfer.  Ophthalmology:  See previous dictation.  No
examination has been performed postoperatively.

PSYCHOSOCIAL:  Mom and dad look forward to Barbara Wheeler moving
closer to home.

CONDITION AT TRANSFER:  Stable.

DISCHARGE DIAGNOSES:  To Saint Francis Hospital Memphis Hospital.  Primary
pediatrician undecided at time of transfer.

CARE RECOMMENDATIONS:  Continue 150 ml per kilogram per day
of breast milk 24 with ProMod.
Medications as listed above which include Diuril 28 mg P.O.
p. g. q 12 hours, which equals 30 mg per kilo per day, K-Ciel
1 mEq P.O. p. g. three times a day which equals 1.5 mEq per
kilo per day, vitamin E 5 units p. g. P.O. daily, ferrous
sulfate .15 ml of 25 ml per ml P.O. p. g. once daily, human
milk fortifier 4 calories per ounce per feeding, MCT oil 4
calories per ounce per feeding, Polycose 4 calories per ounce
per feeding, ProMod 12-29 teaspoon in 50 ml of breast milk,
Similac powder 2 calories per ounce per feeding, Tylenol 30
mg P.O. p. g. q 6 hours postoperatively with the last dose
given on 06-01.

CAR SEAT POSITION SCREENING:  Not done at time of transverse.

STATE NEWBORN SCREEN:  As in previous dictation.

IMMUNIZATIONS RECEIVED:  See previous dictation.  Needs first
set of vaccinations.

FOLLOW UP APPOINTMENT SCHEDULED:  Recommended to be
determined at time of discharge.

DISCHARGE DIAGNOSES:  See previous dictation.
Please include bilateral inguinal hernia repair.



                        [**First Name11 (Name Pattern1) **] Fletcher, 9403
